tema: Have You Ever...? — Experiences (details in past simple)

banco_oraciones.oracion: I have been to Europe. I went to Madrid in 2020.
Ben has broken his arm. He fell off a hammock last year.
Abuela Carmen has won a cumbia competition. It was in 1975.
Kenji has sung karaoke in three languages.
Sofía has never driven a motorcycle.
They haven't met a famous person.
Have you ever been to Europe?
Has Ben ever broken a bone?
Have you ever lost your keys at midnight?
Have you ever been to Europe? Yes, I have. I went in 2020.
Have you ever sung karaoke? No, I haven't.
Has she ever won a prize? Yes, she has. She won it in 1975.

interaccion: Kenji: **Have you ever been** to Europe, Sofía?
Sofía: **Yes, I have.** I **went** to Madrid in 2020, for a design conference.
Ben: **Have you ever broken** a bone? I have... I **broke** my arm last year. I **fell** off a hammock. A hammock, Kenji.
Abuela Carmen: I **have never broken** anything. But **have you ever won** a cumbia competition? I **did** — in 1975.

banner_diagnostico.opcion_a: Have you ever went to Europe?
banner_diagnostico.opcion_b: Have you ever been to Europe?

inicio.definicion_pragmatica: 'Have you ever...?' asks if something happened in your life at least once — and when the answer is yes, you tell the details in past simple: Yes, I have. I went in 2020.

tabla.titulo: The experience question — and the switch to past
filas: Have you ever been to Europe?
Yes, I have. + past simple story
Yes, I have. I went in 2020.
No, I haven't. / I have never + participle
Have you ever won a prize?
She has never driven a motorcycle
perfect = your life · past = the specific moment
I have been to Europe · I went in 2020

ejercicios.pregunta: I have ___ seen snow in my life.

ejercicios.afirmacion: After 'Yes, I have', we tell the details (when, where) in past simple.
'Ever' is used in affirmative sentences: 'I have ever been to Peru'.

ejercicios.texto: Yes, he has. He ___ (break) his arm last year.
Abuela Carmen won the competition. It ___ (be) in 1975.

ejercicios.respuesta: I have never driven a motorcycle

ejercicios.texto_con_error: Have you ever went to Europe?
ejercicios.respuesta: Have you ever been to Europe?

ejercicios.texto_con_error: Yes, I have. I have gone to Madrid in 2020.
ejercicios.respuesta: Yes, I have. I went to Madrid in 2020.

ejercicios.texto_con_error: She has never wons a prize.
ejercicios.respuesta: She has never won a prize.

ejercicios.oracion_base: You have met a famous person.
ejercicios.respuesta: Have you ever met a famous person?
No, I have never seen snow.

ejercicios.oracion_base: Have you ever been to Europe? (Yes — Madrid, 2020)
ejercicios.instruccion: Answer with the short answer + the detail in past simple.
ejercicios.respuesta: Yes, I have. I went to Madrid in 2020.
Kenji has never tried hormigas culonas.

ejercicios.pregunta: Have you ever lost your phone? (short answer)
Have you ever driven a Formula 1 car? (short answer, No)

guion: Kenji: Question game! Have you ever sung karaoke, Sofía?
Sofía: Yes, I have. I sang 'Vivir Mi Vida' at my cousin's wedding in 2023. Twice.
Ben: Have you ever won anything, Kenji?
Kenji: Yes, I have! I won a photography contest when I was fifteen.
Sofía: And you, Abuela? Have you ever met anyone famous?
Abuela Carmen: Yes, I have. I met Gabriel García Márquez at a market in 1982. He bought mangoes. I sold them to him.

comprension.pregunta: When did Sofía sing at the wedding?
What did Abuela Carmen sell to the famous writer?

reading.texto: Kenji's blog, this week: 'Have you ever...? — The interview'. I asked my three favorite people about their lives. Sofía has been to Europe: she went to Madrid in 2020 for a conference and she loved the museums. Ben has broken three bones in his life: he broke the last one when he fell off a hammock — nobody knows how it was possible. And Abuela Carmen? She has met a Nobel Prize winner. 'I met him in 1982,' she told me. 'He bought my mangoes and said they were the best in Colombia.' I have never met anyone famous... but I have met Abuela Carmen, and I think that counts.

preguntas.afirmacion: Ben has never broken a bone.
Abuela Carmen met the writer in 1982.
Kenji has met many famous people.
